content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is happening right now! It started on June 11, 2026 and will finish on July 19, 2026. This is a very special World Cup because for the first time ever, three countries are hosting together: the United States, Mexico, and Canada! Matches are being played in 16 different cities across all three countries. Another first - 48 teams are competing this year, more than ever before, playing a total of 104 matches over 39 days. The opening match was played in Mexico City at the famous Estadio Azteca. The final will be played on July 19 at a stadium in New Jersey, and it will even have a halftime show like the Super Bowl! Argentina, led by their captain, came in as the defending champion from the 2022 World Cup, hoping to win again.

NASA has exciting and changing plans for exploring Mars in 2026! In January 2026, NASA's old plan to bring Martian rock samples back to Earth was paused because it was going to cost too much money and take too long. Instead, NASA made a brand new plan! In June 2026, NASA announced a partnership with a private rocket company called Relativity Space to build a new Mars mission called Aeolus. Aeolus will use special instruments to study Martian wind, dust, temperature, and clouds every single day from space. This information will help scientists understand Mars better and make it safer for future robots and astronauts to land there. Meanwhile, NASA's older Mars spacecraft called MAVEN, which studied the Martian atmosphere for over 11 years, came to the end of its mission in 2026 after losing contact with Earth.
